from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class UserRecord:
    id: int
    name: str
    is_active: bool

def parse_user_record(line) -> UserRecord:
    # Correct: return a named structure (dataclass or NamedTuple) for clarity and maintainability
    parts = line.split(",")
    return UserRecord(id=int(parts[0]), name=parts[1], is_active=(parts[2] == "True"))
